channel.title: I'm Mauricio Alpuin
channel.description: Recent content on I'm Mauricio Alpuin

item.title: Journey from your laptop to a CI/CD to Kubernetes - Part 2
Speech to Text - Transcribe your WhatsApp audios
item.description: The other day I was playing with the openai library that transcribes and translates audios, this library is call whisper and there is a package for python. But I really wanted to use it for something else. Almost every day, I receive Whatsapp audios when I am in the middle of a meeting. So I decided to write some code to integrate the transcriber library into the Whatsapp API. And this is the Speech to Text Bot.

item.title: Journey from your laptop to a CI/CD to Kubernetes
item.description: In today’s post I want to write something that came to my mind when I was trying to write code. In particular I was trying to remember how to write code again, so I started to write some kind of API with Flask. Many people love Flask for its simplicity and I wanted to give it a try to see how easy it was to create a functional API from scratch.

item.title: Install Netdata on Microk8s
item.description: This post is a real quick note for myself… I wanted to install netdata to monitoring my homeserver. I think that is the perfect tool for a real time information of your server. When I started to look for the best monitoring tool on the internet, everyone, literally, everyone suggested grafana with prometheus, which I then tried. I think that it is an amaizing tool with a high potential to do whatever you want, but it was not what I was looking for.

item.title: Pi-Hole - DNS and AdBlocker
item.description: This application was the first one that I wanted to try when I installed and configured my personal server. As I wrote in my last post, I’ve installed and configured a personal server at home to run some applications for my personal use, so my next step was this really popular application pi-hole. pi-hole is an opensource and free software that offers a DNS, DHCP and an incredible Ad-blocker (it has other additional functions but the ones that I mention are the core).

item.title: My Home Server
item.description: I finally decided to buy a mini pc to start playing with some basics things that we don’t really need but that are really helpful when you are surfing the web from home. The main reason for doing that was because I’m thinking to move my personal blog to my own hosting, MY HOME SERVER. Technology/Software that I have used: Mini Pc Ubuntu Server 22.04 Microk8s Metallb Heimdall Application Dashboard Here is a diagram of how my home server network and components are working right now.

item.title: Install Kubernetes from scratch
item.description: On my way to get the Kubernetes Administrator Certificate (CKA) I decided to install my own Kubernetes cluster from scratch to get a better understanding about the components behind a cluster. In this tutorial we are going to use the following components in order to install our own cluster and play with it. Kubernetes Docker Terraform DigitalOcean Flannel Ubuntu I’m going to assume that you have already read my latest post and you have your DigitalOcean account or you have already had three virtual machines that we could use for installing and configuring our cluster.

item.title: Deploy your own hosted Ghost Blog on Kubernetes - Part 2
item.description: In the first part of this tutorial we have created our kubernetes cluster and have deployed our Ghost Blog accessible over internet. In this second part we are going to be focused on applying an SSL configuration with Letsencrypt in order to publish our website with valid certificates for all users. Step 1 - Install and configure the Cert Manager 🔗Cert Manager is the responsable tool to add, manage and issue the certificates in the Kubernetes cluster.

item.title: Deploy your own hosted Ghost Blog on Kubernetes - Part 1
item.description: I’ve started to use Ghost as a platform to write my nerdy post, because I would like to use it as my personal notebook, but I want to use it in a good way to contribute to the community as well (because everything is possible googling). But this time, I decided to host my own blog and do all the work from scratch and I want to share what I’m learning while doing it.
